full_prompt: Design a single-part monolithic main chassis skid for a NASA Super Heavy Logistics Transport rover per the NASA 'Super Heavy Logistics Transport for the Moon and Mars' open-CAD challenge. The rover transports a 100 metric-ton pressurized logistics module (habitat-scale) across Lunar and Martian terrain, rotating the module >= 180 deg in place for offload alignment, and traversing at least 500 m of 20 deg grade without stopping. Only the chassis skid (central structural weldment-like monolithic plate) is the subject of this prompt; wheels, motors, and hydraulics are out of scope. Geometric constraints: skid bounding footprint 10 m x 6 m x 0.8 m deep; four corner wheel-attachment bosses each 0.6 m x 0.6 m x 0.4 m with 8x M30 tapped holes in a 300 mm x 300 mm pattern; central rotating payload interface is a 4.0 m diameter slewing-ring mount (bolt circle 3.8 m OD with 72 bolts) through which the 100 t module is attached; four tie-down hardpoints on the perimeter (rated 250 kN each). Material: Ti-6Al-4V Grade 5 annealed (E = 113.8 GPa, yield = 880 MPa, ultimate = 950 MPa, density = 4430 kg/m^3). Environments: Lunar ambient -173 C to +127 C; Martian -125 C to +20 C. Load cases: (R1) Static 100 t payload in Lunar gravity (1.62 m/s^2): payload mass x g = 100,000 kg x 1.62 = 162 kN downward uniform on slewing-ring bolt circle. (R2) Static 100 t payload in Mars gravity (3.71 m/s^2) = 371 kN downward. (R3) Driving on 20 deg slope in Mars gravity: vehicle + payload 120 t x 3.71 x sin(20 deg) = 152 kN longitudinal shear at slewing ring + 120,000 x 3.71 x cos(20 deg) = 418 kN vertical reaction; combined loading at the slewing ring. (R4) Rotational inertia: payload rotated +/-180 deg in 60 s yields omega-dot = pi/60 rad/s^2 applied to 100 t at 4 m radius; torque 100,000 x 0.0524 x 4 = 20.9 kN*m applied to skid through slewing ring. (R5) Dynamic pothole impact: Mars 1.5 m rock strike at one wheel at 5 m/s; lumped as 800 kN vertical shock at one corner boss (0.5 s half-sine). Pass/fail: (R1-R4) max von Mises <= yield / 1.5 = 587 MPa; (R5) max von Mises under dynamic shock <= ultimate / 1.25 = 760 MPa; all cases max deflection at slewing-ring center <= 25 mm (module alignment); skid mass <= 18,000 kg (structural mass budget); first modal frequency with corner bosses hard-mounted >= 8 Hz (avoid coupling with drive motor 5 Hz peak); thermal warp differential between Lunar day (+127 C) and night (-173 C) must produce slewing-ring flatness deviation <= 1.0 mm (structural-thermal CTE check at alpha = 8.6e-6 /K). Primary verification: structural FEA (linear static x4 + dynamic x1) + modal + coupled thermal-structural. Vibration secondary via R6 modal. Fluid, radiation, buckling NOT required. Deliverables: STEP AP242 + Parasolid skid, slewing-ring bolt drawing, and a report listing skid mass in kg, max von Mises per case in MPa, max slewing-ring deflection in mm, first modal frequency in Hz, thermal flatness delta in mm.
short_prompt:
Design a single-part monolithic main chassis skid for a NASA 'Super Heavy Logistics Transport for the Moon and Mars' open-CAD rover. The skid transfers a 100 t pressurized logistics module through a central slewing ring into four corner wheel bosses, must permit >= 180 deg in-place module rotation, and handle a 20 deg Mars grade.

Envelope: skid 10 m x 6 m x 0.8 m deep; four 0.6 x 0.6 x 0.4 m corner bosses with 8 x M30 on 300 mm patterns; 4.0 m diameter slewing-ring bolt circle (3.8 m OD, 72 bolts); four 250 kN perimeter tie-downs.

Factor regime: yield FoS 1.5 for static/rotational cases, ultimate FoS 1.25 for the dynamic pothole, slewing-ring flatness tolerance 1.0 mm on thermal warp.

Load cases.
- Static 100 t in Lunar gravity (1.62 m/s^2): 162 kN on the slewing-ring bolt circle.
- Static 100 t in Mars gravity (3.71 m/s^2): 371 kN.
- Driving 20 deg Mars slope (120 t vehicle+payload): 152 kN longitudinal + 418 kN vertical at the slewing ring.
- Rotational inertia: 100 t rotated +/- 180 deg in 60 s, ~20.9 kN-m torque at the slewing ring.
- Pothole: 800 kN half-sine over 0.5 s at one corner boss (Mars 1.5 m rock strike).
- Thermal: Lunar day +127 C to night -173 C (CTE 8.6e-6 /K).

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: 800 kN half-sine rock-strike shock at one corner requires explicit time integration.

prompt.title: NASA super heavy logistics rover chassis skid
prompt.brief: Ti-6Al-4V monolithic skid, 100 t payload on Moon/Mars, 20 deg slope + 180 deg rotate + 800 kN rock strike.

boundary_conditions.mounting: four corner wheel-attachment bosses fully fixed

exclusions_explained.fluid_analysis: Lunar vacuum; Mars atmosphere negligible aero on skid.
exclusions_explained.buckling_analysis: Skid is bending-stiffness dominated; not driving.

eval_coverage: 100 t payload on two gravity environments
